name: feat-ESE-0015-02
title: Camera and RenderQuad node types
description: Add renderTarget property to Camera nodes and create the new RenderQuad node type. Camera gains: renderTarget ('browser' or texture ID string), aspectRatio, aspectRatioMismatch ('stretch'|'letterbox'|'truncate'), recursionDepth. RenderQuad is a new node type that displays a named render target texture on a quad, with width and height properties. Update node.ts, node-handlers.ts, and view sync to handle the new node type.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Camera node data includes renderTarget, aspectRatio, aspectRatioMismatch, recursionDepth
- RenderQuad node type added to NodeType union
- RenderQuad handler creates a plane mesh and calls setMaterialTexture to bind the render target
- View sync creates renderer objects for RenderQuad nodes
- Existing camera behavior unchanged when renderTarget is 'browser' or omitted
demoDeliverable: Terminal output from unit tests showing camera renderTarget property and RenderQuad node creation.
testingScenarios:
- Create a camera node with renderTarget property, verify it propagates
- Create a RenderQuad node, verify plane mesh created with correct dimensions
- Verify RenderQuad handler calls setMaterialTexture with the right target ID
- Verify existing camera tests still pass (no regression)
stakeholderUnderstanding: Cameras to see, quads to draw. Additionally handling screen vs world space, aspect ratio mismatches, and recursion depth — properties needed for the scenarios in the parent ticket.
